gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The signed two's-complement value of an MSB-first bit string. The empty string is `0`."] # [must_use] pub fn bits_to_int (bits : & [bool]) -> i64 { if bits . is_empty () { return 0 ; } let n = bits . len () ; let mut unsigned : i128 = 0 ; for & b in bits { unsigned = (unsigned << 1) | i128 :: from (b) ; } if bits [0] { unsigned -= 1i128 << n ; } unsigned as i64 }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `n`-bit two's-complement representation of `value`, MSB-first — or `None` if `value` is"] # [doc = " outside `B_n` (explicit out-of-range, never a silent wrap; §2/§4 P4)."] # [must_use] pub fn int_to_bits (value : i64 , n : u32) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if n == 0 { return if value == 0 { Some (Vec :: new ()) } else { None } ; } let n = n as usize ; let lo = - (1i128 << (n - 1)) ; let hi = (1i128 << (n - 1)) - 1 ; let v = i128 :: from (value) ; if v < lo || v > hi { return None ; } let modulus = 1i128 << n ; let u = v . rem_euclid (modulus) ; let mut bits = vec ! [false ; n] ; for (i , slot) in bits . iter_mut () . enumerate () { * slot = (u >> (n - 1 - i)) & 1 == 1 ; } Some (bits) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The **unsigned** magnitude of an MSB-first bit string, exact for `n ≤ 64`. Unlike"] # [doc = " [`bits_to_int`] (which reads the two's-complement/**signed** value), this reads the same bits as"] # [doc = " an unsigned bitvector — RFC-0033 §4.1.1: `Repr::Binary`'s stored value has no signedness;"] # [doc = " signedness is a property of the *op*, not the *value*. The empty string is `0`."] # [must_use] pub fn bits_to_uint (bits : & [bool]) -> u64 { let mut v : u64 = 0 ; for & b in bits { v = (v << 1) | u64 :: from (b) ; } v }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `n`-bit **unsigned** representation of `value`, MSB-first — `None` if `value` does not fit"] # [doc = " the unsigned range `[0, 2^n − 1]` (n ≤ 64) — explicit out-of-range, never a silent"] # [doc = " wrap/truncation. The unsigned counterpart to [`int_to_bits`]."] # [must_use] pub fn uint_to_bits (value : u64 , n : u32) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if n == 0 { return if value == 0 { Some (Vec :: new ()) } else { None } ; } if n < 64 && value >= (1u64 << n) { return None ; } let n = n as usize ; let mut bits = vec ! [false ; n] ; for (i , slot) in bits . iter_mut () . enumerate () { * slot = (value >> (n - 1 - i)) & 1 == 1 ; } Some (bits) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Two's-complement fixed-width multiply of two equal-width `n`-bit two's-complement integers"] # [doc = " (MSB-first), for `n ≤ `[`MUL_MAX_WIDTH`]. `None` when `a.len() != b.len()`, `a.len() >"] # [doc = " MUL_MAX_WIDTH`, or the exact product does not fit `B_n = [−2^(n-1), 2^(n-1) − 1]` — never-silent"] # [doc = " fixed-width overflow (RFC-0033 §4.1.2/§4.1.3; ADR-028 — the shared, signedness-agnostic bit"] # [doc = " pattern, read here under the two's-complement/signed interpretation), the same contract"] # [doc = " [`crate::ternary::mul`] gives the balanced-ternary side."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Implementation.** Both operands round-trip through [`bits_to_int`] into `i64` (exact for `n ≤"] # [doc = " 64`), widen to `i128` for the multiply (`|a|,|b| ≤ 2^63 ⇒ |a·b| ≤ 2^126 « i128::MAX` — the"] # [doc = " product itself never overflows `i128`), then the exact product is range-checked against `B_n`"] # [doc = " before narrowing back through [`int_to_bits`]. This is exact, not an approximation: every step"] # [doc = " up to the final range check is a lossless widening."] # [must_use] pub fn mul (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != b . len () || a . len () > MUL_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return Some (Vec :: new ()) ; } let av = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (a)) ; let bv = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (b)) ; let product = av * bv ; let lo = - (1i128 << (n - 1)) ; let hi = (1i128 << (n - 1)) - 1 ; if product < lo || product > hi { return None ; } int_to_bits (product as i64 , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Unsigned** fixed-width multiply of two equal-width `n`-bit unsigned bitvectors (MSB-first), for"] # [doc = " `n ≤ `[`MUL_MAX_WIDTH`]. `None` when `a.len() != b.len()`, `a.len() > MUL_MAX_WIDTH`, or the exact"] # [doc = " product does not fit `U_n = [0, 2^n − 1]` — never-silent fixed-width **unsigned** overflow"] # [doc = " (RFC-0033 §4.1.2/§4.1.3). This is the unsigned counterpart of [`mul`]: the *bits* of a truncated"] # [doc = " product would be identical either way (multiply is signedness-agnostic on the low `n` bits,"] # [doc = " ADR-028), but the **overflow criterion differs** — an unsigned product is out of range when it"] # [doc = " exceeds `2^n − 1`, distinct from [`mul`]'s signed `B_n` bound (e.g. `3 · 6 = 18` at `n = 4` is"] # [doc = " unsigned-out-of-range `> 15` yet also signed-out-of-range, whereas `5 · 1 = 5` is unsigned-in-range"] # [doc = " `[0,15]` but the signed reading of `0b0101` is `+5` — the criteria coincide only by construction of"] # [doc = " the operands). The unsigned analogue of the [`div_rem`]/[`shr`] unsigned family; the genuinely-"] # [doc = " missing unsigned multiply flagged by `lib/std/math.myc` FLAG-math-1."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Implementation.** Both operands round-trip through [`bits_to_uint`] into `u64` (exact for `n ≤"] # [doc = " 64`), widen to `u128` for the multiply (`a,b ≤ 2^64 − 1 ⇒ a·b ≤ 2^128 − 2^65 + 1 < u128::MAX` — the"] # [doc = " product itself never overflows `u128`), then the exact product is range-checked against `U_n`"] # [doc = " before narrowing back through [`uint_to_bits`]. Exact, not an approximation."] # [must_use] pub fn mul_unsigned (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != b . len () || a . len () > MUL_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return Some (Vec :: new ()) ; } let av = u128 :: from (bits_to_uint (a)) ; let bv = u128 :: from (bits_to_uint (b)) ; let product = av * bv ; if product >= (1u128 << n) { return None ; } uint_to_bits (product as u64 , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Encode a small count `c ∈ [0, n]` as an `n`-bit **MSB-first** unsigned value — arbitrary-width-"] # [doc = " safe. A bit-count never exceeds `n`, and every bit of its representation at position `≥ 64` is"] # [doc = " necessarily `0`, so this sidesteps the `>> ≥ 64` shift UB that [`uint_to_bits`] assumes away for"] # [doc = " its `n ≤ 64` domain — letting the width-preserving bit-manipulation ops below stay uncapped like"] # [doc = " the elementwise family (`bit.and`/`bit.not`), rather than borrowing `mul`'s arithmetic cap."] fn count_to_bits (c : u64 , n : usize) -> Vec < bool > { let mut out = vec ! [false ; n] ; for (i , slot) in out . iter_mut () . enumerate () { let pos = n - 1 - i ; if pos < 64 { * slot = (c >> pos) & 1 == 1 ; } } out }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Population count** of an `n`-bit bitvector — the number of `1` bits, as the `n`-bit unsigned"] # [doc = " count (CU-6; Rust `count_ones`). Total and width-preserving: `popcount ∈ [0, n]` always fits `n`"] # [doc = " bits (`n ≤ 2^n − 1` for `n ≥ 1`; `n = 0` ⇒ `0`). No signedness — a pure bit query."] # [must_use] pub fn popcount (bits : & [bool]) -> Vec < bool > { let c = bits . iter () . filter (| & & b | b) . count () as u64 ; count_to_bits (c , bits . len ()) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Unsigned fixed-width division and remainder of two equal-width `n`-bit bitvectors (MSB-first),"] # [doc = " for `n ≤ `[`DIV_MAX_WIDTH`]. Returns `(quotient, remainder)` such that the Euclidean identity"] # [doc = " holds bit-exactly: `a == quotient * b + remainder` with `0 ≤ remainder < b` (unsigned integer"] # [doc = " division — `quotient = a / b`, `remainder = a % b` — is Euclidean division for unsigned"] # [doc = " operands, unlike the signed case, where truncating and Euclidean division diverge on negative"] # [doc = " operands; RFC-0033 §4.1.2/§4.1.3)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `None` when `a.len() != b.len()`, `a.len() > DIV_MAX_WIDTH`, or `b` is the all-zero bitvector —"] # [doc = " an explicit, never-silent div-by-zero refusal (G2), never a panic or a fabricated result."] # [doc = " Division never overflows for unsigned fixed-width operands (`quotient`/`remainder` are always"] # [doc = " `< 2^n` when they exist, since `a < 2^n`), so width mismatch/cap and div-by-zero are the only"] # [doc = " refusal reasons — there is no separate \"overflow\" case, unlike [`mul`]."] # [must_use] pub fn div_rem (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < (Vec < bool > , Vec < bool >) > { if a . len () != b . len () || a . len () > DIV_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return None ; } let av = bits_to_uint (a) ; let bv = bits_to_uint (b) ; if bv == 0 { return None ; } let q = av / bv ; let r = av % bv ; Some ((uint_to_bits (q , n) ? , uint_to_bits (r , n) ?)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Logical** (unsigned) fixed-width left shift of an `n`-bit bitvector (MSB-first) by a"] # [doc = " **shift-amount operand of the same width and shape** — `shift` is itself read as an unsigned"] # [doc = " `n`-bit bitvector (via [`bits_to_uint`]), for `n ≤ `[`SHIFT_MAX_WIDTH`]. Bits shifted past the"] # [doc = " MSB are dropped (never wrapped/rotated) and zero bits are shifted in at the LSB — the unsigned/"] # [doc = " shared reading (RFC-0033 §4.1.2's `shift` op set; the **arithmetic** (sign-extending) right"] # [doc = " shift is a *distinct* signed op deferred to M-767, per §4.1.2's signedness-split requirement)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `None` when `a.len() != shift.len()`, `a.len() > SHIFT_MAX_WIDTH`, or the shift amount is `>=`"] # [doc = " the width `n` — an explicit, never-silent out-of-range-shift-amount refusal (G2), never UB, a"] # [doc = " silent wrap-around/modulo of the shift amount, or a silently-zeroed result. (At `n == 0` the"] # [doc = " only representable shift amount is `0`, and `0 >= 0`, so `n == 0` always refuses — mirroring"] # [doc = " [`div_rem`]'s `n == 0` div-by-zero refusal.)"] # [must_use] pub fn shl (a : & [bool] , shift : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != shift . len () || a . len () > SHIFT_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return None ; } let k = bits_to_uint (shift) ; if k >= u64 :: from (n) { return None ; } let av = u128 :: from (bits_to_uint (a)) ; let modulus = 1u128 << n ; let result = ((av << k) % modulus) as u64 ; uint_to_bits (result , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Logical** (unsigned, zero-filling) fixed-width right shift — the counterpart to [`shl`], same"] # [doc = " operand shape (`shift` is an unsigned `n`-bit bitvector) and the same cap/refusal contract:"] # [doc = " `None` on a width mismatch, an over-cap width, or a shift amount `>= n` (never UB/wrap/silent,"] # [doc = " including at `n == 0`). (The **arithmetic**/sign-extending right shift is the distinct signed op deferred to"] # [doc = " M-767.)"] # [must_use] pub fn shr (a : & [bool] , shift : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != shift . len () || a . len () > SHIFT_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return None ; } let k = bits_to_uint (shift) ; if k >= u64 :: from (n) { return None ; } let av = bits_to_uint (a) ; let result = av >> k ; uint_to_bits (result , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Two's-complement fixed-width add of two equal-width `n`-bit two's-complement integers (MSB-first),"] # [doc = " for `n ≤ `[`TC_MAX_WIDTH`]. `None` when `a.len() != b.len()`, `a.len() > TC_MAX_WIDTH`, or the"] # [doc = " exact sum does not fit `B_n = [−2^(n-1), 2^(n-1) − 1]` — never-silent fixed-width overflow"] # [doc = " (RFC-0033 §4.1.2/§4.1.3), mirroring [`mul`]'s contract. `a`/`b` are widened to `i128` before"] # [doc = " summing so the addition itself never overflows the intermediate (`|a|,|b| ≤ 2^63` ⇒ `|a+b| ≤"] # [doc = " 2^64 « i128::MAX`); only the final range check against `B_n` can refuse."] # [must_use] pub fn add (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != b . len () || a . len () > TC_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return Some (Vec :: new ()) ; } let av = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (a)) ; let bv = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (b)) ; let sum = av + bv ; let lo = - (1i128 << (n - 1)) ; let hi = (1i128 << (n - 1)) - 1 ; if sum < lo || sum > hi { return None ; } int_to_bits (sum as i64 , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Two's-complement fixed-width subtract (`a − b`) of two equal-width `n`-bit two's-complement"] # [doc = " integers (MSB-first), for `n ≤ `[`TC_MAX_WIDTH`]. gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Two's-complement fixed-width negate (`−a`) of an `n`-bit two's-complement integer (MSB-first), for"] # [doc = " `n ≤ `[`TC_MAX_WIDTH`]. `None` when `a.len() > TC_MAX_WIDTH`, or when the exact negation does not"] # [doc = " fit `B_n` — the classic two's-complement negate-overflow edge: `B_n`'s minimum value `−2^(n-1)`"] # [doc = " has no positive counterpart in `B_n` (its magnitude `2^(n-1)` exceeds the maximum `2^(n-1) − 1`),"] # [doc = " so negating it is an explicit refusal, never a silent wrap back to itself. The genuinely-missing"] # [doc = " member of the shared two's-complement set (§4.1.2) — `add`/`sub`/`mul` were already landed (`kpr`'s"] # [doc = " unsigned `bit.add`/`bit.sub`, reconciled as a *different* family in the module doc comment above,"] # [doc = " and M-887's signed [`mul`]); there is no pre-existing \"negate\" to reconcile against."] # [must_use] pub fn neg (a : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () > TC_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return Some (Vec :: new ()) ; } let av = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (a)) ; let negated = - av ; let lo = - (1i128 << (n - 1)) ; let hi = (1i128 << (n - 1)) - 1 ; if negated < lo || negated > hi { return None ; } int_to_bits (negated as i64 , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Validate the shared signed-division operand contract and decode: equal widths, `n ≤"] # [doc = " `[`DIV_MAX_WIDTH`]` (the same cap as the unsigned [`div_rem`] — both pairs ride the `i64`/`u64`"] # [doc = " codecs' exactness bound), `n > 0`, and a nonzero divisor. Returns the two's-complement operand"] # [doc = " values and the width; `None` on any violated precondition (the caller's `EvalError` layer keeps"] # [doc = " the refusal reasons honestly distinct by re-checking the cheap ones — G2, mirroring how"] # [doc = " [`DIV_MAX_WIDTH`]'s doc comment splits the unsigned pair's reasons)."] fn signed_div_operands (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < (i64 , i64 , u32) > { if a . len () != b . len () || a . len () > DIV_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return None ; } let bv = bits_to_int (b) ; if bv == 0 { return None ; } Some ((bits_to_int (a) , bv , n)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Signed** (two's-complement) fixed-width division of two equal-width `n`-bit two's-complement"] # [doc = " integers (MSB-first), for `n ≤ `[`DIV_MAX_WIDTH`]` — the distinct-named signed counterpart to"] # [doc = " the unsigned [`div_rem`] quotient (RFC-0033 §4.1.2/§4.1.3; ADR-028; M-767). The quotient is"] # [doc = " **truncated toward zero** (SMT-LIB `bvsdiv`; Rust `/` — see the module doc's rounding-convention"] # [doc = " note): `-7 / 2 = -3`, not the floored `-4`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `None` when `a.len() != b.len()`, `a.len() > DIV_MAX_WIDTH`, `b` is zero (explicit div-by-zero,"] # [doc = " never a panic — G2), **or the quotient overflows `B_n`** — unlike unsigned division, signed"] # [doc = " fixed-width division has exactly one overflow case: `B_n`'s minimum `−2^(n-1)` divided by `−1`"] # [doc = " has true quotient `+2^(n-1)`, which exceeds `B_n`'s maximum `2^(n-1) − 1`. That case is an"] # [doc = " explicit, never-silent refusal (RFC-0033 §4.1.3), never a wrap back to the minimum."] # [must_use] pub fn div_signed (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { let (av , bv , n) = signed_div_operands (a , b) ? ; let q = i128 :: from (av) / i128 :: from (bv) ; let q64 = i64 :: try_from (q) . ok () ? ; int_to_bits (q64 , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Signed** (two's-complement) fixed-width remainder — the companion to [`div_signed`], with the"] # [doc = " remainder's **sign following the dividend** (SMT-LIB `bvsrem`; Rust `%`): together they satisfy"] # [doc = " the truncated-division identity `a == div_signed(a,b)·b + rem_signed(a,b)` with `|r| < |b|`"] # [doc = " wherever the quotient exists (property-tested)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `None` on the same operand preconditions as [`div_signed`] (width mismatch, over-cap width,"] # [doc = " div-by-zero). Unlike the quotient, the remainder **never overflows**: `|r| < |b| ≤ 2^(n-1)`"] # [doc = " puts every remainder inside `B_n`, so `rem_signed(min, −1) = Some(0)` succeeds even though"] # [doc = " `div_signed(min, −1)` refuses — the exact result fits, and refusing an in-range exact result"] # [doc = " would be an over-refusal §4.1.3 does not ask for (the divergence from Rust's `checked_rem`,"] # [doc = " which refuses on the hardware's paired-instruction behavior, is deliberate and documented)."] # [must_use] pub fn rem_signed (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { let (av , bv , n) = signed_div_operands (a , b) ? ; let r = i128 :: from (av) % i128 :: from (bv) ; int_to_bits (r as i64 , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Arithmetic** (sign-extending) fixed-width right shift — the distinct-named signed counterpart"] # [doc = " to the logical [`shr`] (RFC-0033 §4.1.2's signedness-split `shift` op set; ADR-028's SMT-LIB"] # [doc = " `bvashr`/`bvlshr` split; M-767). Same operand shape and refusal contract as [`shr`]: the shift"] # [doc = " amount is itself an unsigned `n`-bit bitvector (read via [`bits_to_uint`]), and `None` on a"] # [doc = " width mismatch, an over-cap width (`n > `[`SHIFT_MAX_WIDTH`]`), or a shift amount `>= n`"] # [doc = " (explicit, never-silent — never UB, a wrapped shift amount, or an implicit \"fill with the sign"] # [doc = " bit for any amount\" extension; at `n == 0` every amount refuses, as in [`shr`])."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Copies of the **sign bit** (the MSB) are shifted in where [`shr`] shifts in zeros, so the"] # [doc = " result equals `⌊a / 2^k⌋` under the two's-complement reading (floor, i.e. toward −∞ — the"] # [doc = " standard arithmetic-shift semantics; e.g. `-1 >> k = -1` for every in-range `k`). The result"] # [doc = " always fits `B_n` (a magnitude never grows under an arithmetic right shift), so shifting never"] # [doc = " overflows — the refusal reasons are exactly the operand preconditions above."] # [must_use] pub fn shr_signed (a : & [bool] , shift : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != shift . len () || a . len () > SHIFT_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return None ; } let k = bits_to_uint (shift) ; if k >= u64 :: from (n) { return None ; } let av = bits_to_int (a) ; let result = av >> k ; int_to_bits (result , n) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " **Signed** (two's-complement) total order over two equal-width bitvectors — the distinct-named"] # [doc = " signed counterpart to the D1 comparison's unsigned-magnitude order on `Binary` (RFC-0033"] # [doc = " §4.1.2: ordering differs by signedness, so it MUST be a distinct named op; ADR-028's"] # [doc = " `bvslt`/`bvult` split; M-767). `None` on a width mismatch (the caller refuses explicitly —"] # [doc = " never a silent ordering); equal-width operands always order (`Some`), the zero-width bitvector"] # [doc = " comparing `Equal` (`B_0 = {0}`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Width-unbounded and purely structural** (no `DIV_MAX_WIDTH`-style cap): the two's-complement"] # [doc = " order is the unsigned lexicographic order with the sign bit's polarity flipped — compare"] # [doc = " `(¬a[0], a[1..])` against `(¬b[0], b[1..])` — so no integer decode (and hence no width cap)"] # [doc = " is involved, exactly as the unsigned D1 order compares the raw bits. Agreement with"] # [doc = " [`bits_to_int`]'s value order is property-tested on the decodable domain (`n ≤ 64`)."] # [must_use] pub fn cmp_signed (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Ordering > { if a . len () != b . len () { return None ; } if a . is_empty () { return Some (Ordering :: Equal) ; } Some ((! a [0] , & a [1 ..]) . cmp (& (! b [0] , & b [1 ..]))) }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The largest non-negative integer magnitude IEEE-754 binary64 represents **exactly** — every"] # [doc = " integer in `[0, 2^53]` has an exact binary64 encoding; `2^53 + 1` does not (ADR-040 §2.4, the"] # [doc = " classic \"a double loses integer precision past `2^53`\" bound)."] pub const FLOAT_EXACT_MAX : u64 = 1u64 << 53 ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const FLOAT_EXACT_MAX` — initializer is not a decidable non-negative integer literal or a known workspace-floor path (e.g. `RecursionBudget::DEFAULT_DEPTH_LIMIT`); no const item production in the grammar, and co-emit refuses to invent a value (ORACLE-R1 A4 / VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " `Binary{N} → Float`, checked-exact (ADR-040 §2.4; CU-3). Reads `bits` as the **unsigned**"] # [doc = " magnitude ([`bits_to_uint`], sign-free per ADR-028); `None` when `bits.len()` exceeds"] # [doc = " [`FLOAT_CONV_MAX_WIDTH`] or the magnitude exceeds [`FLOAT_EXACT_MAX`] — never a silent lossy"] # [doc = " round (that direction is a reified swap, not this prim; see the module note above)."] # [must_use] pub fn checked_uint_to_f64 (bits : & [bool]) -> Option < f64 > { if bits . len () > FLOAT_CONV_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let magnitude = bits_to_uint (bits) ; if magnitude > FLOAT_EXACT_MAX { return None ; } Some (magnitude as f64) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " `Float → Binary{M}`, never-silent (ADR-040 §2.4; CU-3). `None` when `value` is NaN, ±inf,"] # [doc = " negative (`Binary` is unsigned/sign-free — ADR-028), has a nonzero fractional part (never a"] # [doc = " silent truncation — G2), its magnitude exceeds [`FLOAT_EXACT_MAX`] (so the value itself is not a"] # [doc = " binary64-exact integer — no cast could be trusted), or does not fit the unsigned `m`-bit target"] # [doc = " (`m` capped at [`FLOAT_CONV_MAX_WIDTH`], mirroring the reverse direction)."] # [must_use] pub fn checked_f64_to_uint (value : f64 , m : u32) -> Option < u64 > { if (m as usize) > FLOAT_CONV_MAX_WIDTH || ! value . is_finite () || value < 0.0 || value . fract () != 0.0 || value > FLOAT_EXACT_MAX as f64 { return None ; } let magnitude = value as u64 ; if m < 64 && magnitude >= (1u64 << m) { return None ; } Some (magnitude) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Reduce `value` modulo `2^n` and encode it as the `n`-bit two's-complement bit pattern"] # [doc = " (MSB-first) — the wrapping/modular reduction. Unlike [`int_to_bits`], this never refuses: every"] # [doc = " `i128` value maps to exactly one `n`-bit pattern. `n == 0` maps everything to the empty pattern"] # [doc = " (`B_0 = {0}`)."] fn wrap_to_bits (value : i128 , n : u32) -> Vec < bool > { if n == 0 { return Vec :: new () ; } let n = n as usize ; let modulus = 1i128 << n ; let u = value . rem_euclid (modulus) ; let mut bits = vec ! [false ; n] ; for (i , slot) in bits . iter_mut () . enumerate () { * slot = (u >> (n - 1 - i)) & 1 == 1 ; } bits }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Two's-complement fixed-width **wrapping** add (RFC-0034 §10; CU-5): the sum reduced modulo"] # [doc = " `2^n` into `B_n` instead of refusing out-of-range (contrast [`add`]). `None` only on a"] # [doc = " structural mismatch — `a.len() != b.len()` or an over-[`TC_MAX_WIDTH`] width — never on range."] # [must_use] pub fn wrapping_add (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != b . len () || a . len () > TC_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; let av = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (a)) ; let bv = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (b)) ; Some (wrap_to_bits (av + bv , n)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Two's-complement fixed-width **wrapping** subtract (`a − b`, RFC-0034 §10; CU-5). Same"] # [doc = " structural-only refusal contract as [`wrapping_add`]; the difference wraps modulo `2^n` rather"] # [doc = " than refusing out-of-range (contrast [`sub`])."] # [must_use] pub fn wrapping_sub (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != b . len () || a . len () > TC_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; let av = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (a)) ; let bv = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (b)) ; Some (wrap_to_bits (av - bv , n)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Two's-complement fixed-width **wrapping** multiply (RFC-0034 §10; CU-5). Same structural-only"] # [doc = " refusal contract as [`wrapping_add`] (mirroring [`mul`]'s `MUL_MAX_WIDTH` cap); the product"] # [doc = " wraps modulo `2^n` rather than refusing out-of-range (contrast [`mul`])."] # [must_use] pub fn wrapping_mul (a : & [bool] , b : & [bool]) -> Option < Vec < bool > > { if a . len () != b . len () || a . len () > MUL_MAX_WIDTH { return None ; } let n = a . len () as u32 ; if n == 0 { return Some (Vec :: new ()) ; } let av = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (a)) ; let bv = i128 :: from (bits_to_int (b)) ; Some (wrap_to_bits (av * bv , n)) }
